text:
The Black Whale (, Burakku Hoēru) is a class of large transport vessels built for the Kakin Empire's expedition to the New Continent. The first ship in the class, the "Black Whale 1", can carry 200,000 passengers and serves as the main grounds for Kakin's current succession contest. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 342 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 348 

History

Succession Contest arc

King Nasubi declared that Kakin would build 20 Black Whales in one year, promising to send 100 million people to the "Dark Continent" within five years. Since the first ship's capacity is only 200,000, it can be estimated that the building rate of these ships or the capacity of later classes will increase considerably. 

In front of a crowd of thousands, the Black Whale 1 begins its voyage to the New Continent. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 359 

Description

The Black Whale 1 is 1,500 meters long, 800 meters tall, and 800 meters wide. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 398 Its eyes can function as spotlights in the dark. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 366 

The interior of the Black Whale is simple since it was specialized for transport, reducing the production time and costs. Since the vessel is too massive to be tied up at a dock, airships and ferries are used to transport people and supplies to and from a seaport. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 

Black Whale 1

The Black Whale 1 is the commemorative first ship of the Black Whale class, scheduled to set out on the first voyage to the New Continent. It carries many notable figures, including the Kakin royal family, representatives of the V6, and members of the Hunter Association, many of whom are expecting to extend their journey farther to the Dark Continent. Civilians make up the bulk of passengers, chosen through a lottery with roughly 1 in 1,300 odds of winning. 

The official voyage to the New Continent will be two months long; the Black Whale will leave humanity's normal navigational and fishing waters within three weeks. Then, it will refuel and have a final check performed before continuing into unchartered waters, where various extreme weather phenomena and flying creatures are expected, necessitating the closing of the top dome. Dealers at general stores on Tier 1 can order rare items delivered to the Black Whale via large drones, cargo blimps, or high-speed boats. The deadline for ordering on the last air-shipment is the fourteenth day of the voyage, and the deadline via high-speed boat is three days before then. Depending on the wealth and connections of the customer, smaller items can be ordered past the final deadline. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 392 

The habitable areas of the Black Whale 1 are split into five tiers, connected to the hull by a large number of massive springs. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 406 The five tiers of the Black Whale 1 effectively act as divisors of passengers by wealth and social class; the upper two tiers are separated from the lower three by a thick bulkhead between Tiers 2 and 3. Restrictions on movement between the upper two tiers is unclear, but movement between the lower three tiers through the Central Passages (, Chūō Renraku Tsūro) require tickets, Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 officially from the Royal Army, though the Mafia have their own set of ticket-punchers. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 377 The on-board announcement system is seemingly set up to encourage this social divide; announcements are broadcast-able across all channels, but announcements concerning the upper tiers are generally not broadcast to the lower tiers. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 408 

For the duration of the voyage, the Black Whale 1 operates under martial law by the Kakin Royal Army. The throughways dividing the tiers are guarded by the Royal Army and the bulkhead separating Tiers 2 and 3 can only be opened from the Tier 2 side in case of emergencies. These measures, including the ticket requirement for movement between the lower tiers, is likely a result of the ship being under martial law. Mizaistom estimates that there are roughly 2,000 guards and royal army soldiers on board. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 380 Despite the seemingly harsh military measures taken on board, Kakin seems to not have an interest in managing the situation properly. Even before departure, Mizaistom stated that the number of crimes occurring in the lower tiers were higher than the Zodiacs initially anticipated; crimes include ticket fraud, theft, racial conflicts, drunken brawls, identity theft, assault, and false reports. By the fourth day, the situation was severe enough that he proposed moving reorganizing 800 of the upper tiers' 1,400 guards and soldiers to the lower tiers. 

Perhaps the most important event for the Kakin Empire that takes place on board the Black Whale 1 is their generational Succession Contest, where the Kakin Empire's fourteen Princes covertly battle to the death, with the sole survivor to be King Nasubi's official successor.

Passenger List

For a complete list of all official and unofficial named passengers, see :Category:Black Whale 1 Passengers 
For a list of all Kakin bodyguards and Mafia members who appeared in the story, see Kakin Empire 

The Black Whale 1's passengers are divided into five separate tiers (some of which are further subdivided into decks) according to their wealth and social class: Tier 1 is for the royal family, V5 politicians, and industry dignitaries, Tier 2 for celebrities and the wealthy, while the lower three tiers are zoned for general passengers. 
Tiers 1 and 2 accommodate roughly 20,000 passengers, while Tiers 3 through 5 contain the remaining 180,000. The 20,000 passengers in the upper tier include 1,000 doctors, 1,400 guards and royal army soldiers, and the rest are made up by the royal family, V5 politicians, industry dignitaries, celebrities, the wealthy, Kakin Mafia bosses and their staff (the last two might not be official passengers). 
The lower three tiers only have 600 guards and Royal Army soldiers, with Tiers 4 and 5 relying on the Mafia to maintain public order. The majority of people there are general passengers who boarded the ship in the hope of making a fresh start on the New Continent. 
Several unofficial passengers boarded the ship while avoiding registration on the Royal Army's passenger list. At least 500 Mafia associates, including fringe members, are unofficial passengers listed on the Mafia member files. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 390 On the other hand, all 23 members of the Heil-Ly Family boarded the ship as normal civilians. 

The number of medical clinics ended up being a fifth of what had been planned, and only 1/15 of the doctors boarded the ship. There are three clinics on Tier 3, two on Tier 4 and 5, and Tier 5 is without a dedicated doctor. Consequently, Cheadle was forced to hire more personnel and reorganize the staff. 

Since there are 2,000 guards and Royal Army soldiers among the 200,000 passengers, there should ideally be one guard for every 100 civilians. In reality, Tiers 1 and 2 have 70% of the total guards, while the lower tiers have one guard for every 300 civilians. In order to stop the murders occurring on Tier 3 and to remove the passengers' source of anxiety, Mizaistom proposed reorganizing the Royal Army and sending 800 soldiers from Tiers 1 and 2 to the lower tiers. 

Tier 1

Tier 1 (1, Is-sō) is a separate cruise ship situated in a large pool on top of the Black Whale vessel. It accommodates the royal family, V5 politicians, industry dignitaries, and the bosses of Kakin's three largest Mafia families. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 371 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 378 The residences of the Mafia bosses are in equivalent locations to those of the higher Queens, with their luggage allowances second only to the King's. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 384 

Currently, there are 800 guards and Royal Army soldiers roaming the halls around its living quarters, 150 of whom are Provisional Hunters with limited licenses for the voyage. The tier serves as the main "hunting ground" for Kakin's succession contest. 

King's Living Quarters

The King's Living Quarters (, Ō Kyojū-ku) is the residence of King Nasubi. It is separated from the Princes' living quarters by the banquet hall. The most noticeable features of the living room are its patterned floors, ornate furniture, and various paintings hanging on the walls. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

 
Outline of tier 1 quarters.png|Location
Chap 382 - King's living room.png|The King's Living Room
Chap 382 - Princes Living Quarters Gate.png|The King's Living Quarters Gate
 

Princes' Living Quarters

The Princes' Living Quarters (, Ōji Kyojū-ku) are a large area on Tier 1. Each of the fourteen princes uses one of fourteen identical rooms as their own living quarters, numbered 10xx where the last two digits represent the Princes' order (The 3 rd Prince has Room 1003; the 12 th Prince has 1012, etc.). The fourteen rooms are arranged in two rows of seven rooms, with entrances opening outwards instead of to the entrances of other rooms. Assuming the banquet hall is north of the rooms, the even-numbered rooms are on the left, and the odd-numbered rooms are on the right, with the higher Princes being closer to the banquet hall. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 

The rooms do not share walls and have walkable corridors between adjacent rooms. The rooms are "back to back", and so the main doors to each quarter open away from the other rooms. The fourteen rooms are enclosed within another wall with fourteen entrances, one in front of each door to a Prince's living quarters. The main door to a living quarter opens to a short corridor leading to its living room. Yet another wall surrounds this outer wall and isolates the Princes' Living quarters from the rest of the deck, only accessible via an entrance opposite the banquet hall, guarded by soldiers from the Royal Army. 

Living Quarters

Each of a Prince's Living Quarters has the same layout. The Prince's Master Bedroom is at the back of the living quarters, with its own shower and washroom nearby. In front of it is a living space, which takes up most of the middle of a living quarter. Near the front, there is a kitchen and dining room for meals, and a servants' quarters for the Prince's servants to reside in. 

On the right side of the Princes' living quarters (the side with the odd-numbered princes), the VVIP Private Recreational Facilities (ブイビップ, Buibippu Ōji Sen'yō Goraku Shisetsu) are made available for the Princes, since they can only be accessed from the same entrance. 

 
princes living quarters.png|Princes' Living Quarters' Outline
Outline of tier 1 quarters.png|Location
Princes' Living Quarters Map.png|Individual Room Map
 

VVIP Living Quarters

The VVIP Living Quarters (ブイビップ, Buibippu Kyojū-ku) are separated from the banquet hall by the Princes' living quarters. One way to gain access to go through the corridor bordering the wall of the Princes' quarters, passing by the living quarters of soldiers and associates and the passageway to lower tiers. It is the residence of the VIP outside of the royal family who reside on Tier 1 (including V5 politicians, industry dignitaries, and the bosses of Kakin's three largest Mafia families). It is likely that all three Mafia family bosses have a connection to Tier 2, but the only one that has been confirmed to exist is a cross-bridge from inside Xi-Yu boss Onior's residence. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 410 

Princes Benjamin and Camilla are currently confined to this area after Cleapatro's decision at their hearing. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 376 Benjamin's residence within the VVIP area has also been labeled 'Room 1001'; Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 402 it is unclear if his former residence continues to be labeled as such or not.

Queens' Living Quarters

Each Queen has their own room on Tier 1, though where it is on Tier 1 is unclear. 1 st Queen Unma Hui Guo Rou is the only one to have been seen with a room so far, and she has Room 01. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 403 

Soldiers/Associates' Living Quarters

The Soldiers/Associates' Living Quarters (・, Heishi/Junkaiin Seikatsu-ku) are designated for the Kakin soldiers and about 150 Provisional Hunters who passed the 289th Hunter Exam with limited licenses only for the voyage. They can be reached from the left side of the Princes' living quarters which is heavily guarded by soldiers. Following these quarters is a passageway that leads to the lower tiers. 

Banquet Hall

The Banquet Hall (ホール, Enkai Hōru), also known as the Ceremony Hall or Reception Hall, is an assembly room for social gatherings that lies between the King's and Princes' living quarters. The departure ceremony took place here on the first day of the voyage. On the subsequent Sundays, the hall's main function was changed to host dinner parties to which the Princes and dignitaries are invited. Each Prince is asked to enter or leave the hall through a passageway at prescribed times. 

The banquet hall is furnished with many dining tables and provides a buffet restaurant for its guests. King Nasubi's throne lies on a raised platform on one end of the hall, while a central stage for announcers and different performers is placed on the other end. The program acts are separated into two halves and played over the intercoms on Tiers 1 and 2. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 383 Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 385

Jail

Beyond's Holding Cell (べヨンド, Beyondo Kōsoku-shitsu) is used to house Beyond Netero during the entirety of the voyage in accordance with his contract with the V5, which states that he will stay in a room under 24-hour watch, with chaperones accompanying him on outings, and that his whereabouts will be monitored via an anklet with a tracking device, with any future communications being managed and monitored. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 33, Chapter 345 The holding cell is very small, only able to include a urinal and a bed for Beyond whose right arm is restrained by a manacle that is bolted into the cell's wall. On the first day of the voyage, there were three Zodiacs watching him, with Kanzai questioning the system. Saccho internally agrees that three aren't needed when Beyond has no motive to escape for now, saying that they will suggest a rotation to Mizaistom so that one of them can support the Royal Army. 

A first-class cell was used to detain Camilla after she assaulted Benjamin and killed two of his guards. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 Other criminals and suspects are taken into custody in standard cells before facing a court-martial. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 372 

 
Chap 359 - Beyond in his cell.png|Beyond's Holding Cell
Chap 373 - Camilla's first class cell.png|Camilla's First-Class Cell
Tuffdy's cell.png|Tuffdy's Standard Cell
Prison Corridor.png|Corridor to Standard Cells
 

Supreme Court

The Supreme Court (, Saikō Saibankan-shitsu) is part of Kakin's independent judicial branch. It is the highest court on board the Black Whale, having the ultimate judicial authority. Cleapatro resides there as the Supreme Magistrate in charge of conducting trials, deciding what evidence is admissible, and issuing rulings based upon the facts presented. 

 
Chap 376 - Benjamin and Camilla inside the courtroom.png|Supreme Court
 

Princes' Burial Chamber

The Princes' Burial Chamber is a large room containing fourteen caskets used to hold the deceased bodies of the Princes who participated in the succession contest. It is currently unknown where the chamber is located on the ship, but it is presumably on Tier 1, given its connection to the Princes and the succession contest. 

The room comprises of a large capsule in the center of the room surrounded by fourteen caskets spaced evenly apart, and a spot between each casket and the central capsule that lights up whenever a casket is occupied. Two pots lie near the capsule's edge, opposing each other. The floor and walls bear a strong resemblance to the inscriptions found at Greed Island's starting point. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 13, Chapter 127 Because of the inscription and the central chamber, it can be speculated that the room has a purpose beyond simply being a burial chamber.

The number of caskets lines up nicely with the number of participants in the succession contest, however, if there is to be a winner, there should be one fewer casket than there are participants. It is unclear if Nasubi intends for all Princes to die, or if that each Prince has an assigned casket which does not necessarily need to be filled, or possibly even that Nasubi expects one of the caskets to be occupied by him. It is also unclear what happens should the chambers not be fully filled, since there is no direct confirmation that Prince Kacho's body was recovered from the lifeboat that's out at sea. 

Halkenburg's body is to be transported from Tier 3's general hospital to the morgue on Tier 1. It is possible that this is the morgue in question. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

 
Chap 371 - Kakin Tree.png|The Princes' Burial Chamber
 

Lifeboat Launch Site

The Lifeboat Launch Site (の, Kyūmei-bōto no Hasshajō) can be accessed from an emergency door secured by a keypad lock and guarded by Kakin Royal Army soldiers. It took Keeney and Princes Kacho and Fugetsu two minutes to run there from the banquet hall's passageway. The closed lifeboats are placed in a waterway and can be launched from a nearby control unit. They exit the ship from an outlet hole just above the Black Whale's waterline. 

 
Chap 383 - Lifeboat Launch Site.png|Lifeboat Launch Site
Chap 383 - Lifeboat controls.png|Lifeboat Controls
Chap 383 - Black Whale lifeboats interior.png|Lifeboat Interior
Chap 383 - Waterway Outlet.png|Waterway Outlet
 

Casino

Tier 1 has a VIP casino, where Bonolenov comes across Hisoka. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 This appears to be part of what Hinrigh refers to as the recreation area. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 393 There are many slot machines present and several staffed poker tables, where some unconventional variants of poker can be played. Employees dressed in bunny outfits are also seen with drinks for patrons. 

Tier 2

Tier 2 (2, Ni-sō) is at the highest level of the Black Whale's hull and accommodates celebrities and the wealthy. A thick bulkhead is utilized to separate it from the third and lower tiers, and can only be opened in emergencies from the second-tier side. Tier 2 is a part of what Balsamilco designates as the "hunting grounds" for his men during Kakin's succession contest. Currently, there are 600 guards and Royal Army soldiers stationed on this tier. 

Theater Venue

The Theater Venue (シアター, Shiatā Kaijō) is a small auditorium with a large projection screen and several loges. After the departure of the Black Whale 1, a presenter explains the details of the two-month voyage to a small crowd. 

 
theatre venue.png|Theater Venue
 

Justice Bureau

The Justice Bureau (, Shihō-kyoku) Chapter 402 introduced the term , Shihō-shō, which Viz translated as "Justice Department". It is currently unclear why the two terms exist, since there has been no clear indication thus far that the two terms refer to different entities. On this website, the term used is "Ministry of Justice", in order to better distinguish between when the two terms are used. is a five-story building and part of Kakin's independent judicial branch. Kaiser described it as the most neutral institution on board the ship. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 400 After the incident at the Sunday banquet, Melody, Kacho (actually Kacho's Guardian Spirit Beast), and Fugetsu are being held there for questioning by Kaiser. Melody also deduced that Kaiser was keeping her there to protect her from the dangerous Princes. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 386 During the voyage, Steiner and Peuckert from the IPA were temporarily relocated to the Bureau's investigation department. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 389 

Kaiser has a small office filled with several full bookcases. The bureau has multiple interrogation rooms and a lavishly furnished VIP Witness Protection Area (のエリア, Bippuyō no Shōnin Hōgo Eria), which Kaiser describes as the safest place on the ship. The bureau also has at least one room where visitations can happen. They can only last 10 minutes, and all conversations are recorded. A public defender is also present since the exchanges might be used in a court of law. Panels of glass act as a barrier, and each side has a drawer if items need to be given or taken. 

 
Chap 386 - Justice Bureau.png|Justice Bureau
Chap 386 - Justice Bureau Lobby.png|Lobby
Chap 386 - Justice Bureau Visiting Room.png|Interrogation Room
Chap 400 - VIP Witness Protection Area.png|VIP Witness Protection Area
Chap 400 - Visitation Room.png|Visitation Room
 

Between Tiers 2 and 3

Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout

The Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout (エイ＝イのしアジト, Ei i Ikka no Kakushi Ajito) is located between Tiers 2 and 3, Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 409 and is the family's main base of operations. The Heil-Ly Family have a registered hideout on Tier 3 which they have largely abandoned. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 394 Although its exact location is unknown, the closing of the bulkhead between Tiers 2 and 3 can be heard from inside the hideout. 

The Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout has five entrances, one of which Morena refers to as "Door C". Likely through member Voconte's Nen ability, the Heil-Ly have the ability to teleport to at least Room 3101 and likely Room 3131 as well. It is unclear if these two rooms are also considered entrances by Morena, and if Room 3131 is "Door C". In a conversation between Notre and Soufflé in Chapter 394, Notre hints that Room 3131 can be accessed by stepping through the living room door from the shower room. However, when Hinrigh and Nobunaga are forced through this teleport in Chapter 399, they find themselves in Room 3101. 

The Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout consists of several rooms. One of them is a large square shower room, which one can teleport into through Room 3101. This is one of the rooms Nobunaga refers to as "Rooms A and B" in a floor plan he gives to Phinks and Feitan. It is dimly lit by small lights on the walls, with dirty, blood-stained floor tiles and dirty, graffitied, Nen-reinforced walls. This room has seven doors across three walls, five on one wall, one on the left wall of that five-door wall, and one on the opposite of the five-door wall, which people teleported in through Room 3101 initially face. A pair of taps with hoses and brushing/scrubbing utensils lean on the final wall. Three of the five doors on the five-door wall are marked with gender signs, two male and one female, and lead to toilets, without showers or sinks. One of the male-marked doors has a urinal in bad condition behind it, and the other has a regular toilet. The female-marked door has a toilet with a flower pot on top of it behind it. The two doors on the right of the toilets are a bathroom and a shower room, both with mats for drying feet in front of the doors. The shower room is also referred to as the processing room by Notre in a conversation with Soufflé. 

The door on the left of the five-door wall opens to a laundry room. The laundry room appears to be as large as the shower room, but is well-used and filled with furniture and appliances to help do laundry. In the center of the room lies a table with human remains on it. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 38, Chapter 399 The laundry is also referred to as "disposal" by Notre in a conversation with Soufflé. 

The door opposite of the five-door wall connects to a brightly-lit living room, and is the other room Nobunaga referred to with "Rooms A and B". A large table is in the middle of the room, large enough to seat four people each on its longer sides, and there is a couch against the left wall. Above the couch, there is a dartboard, and a bookcase in the corner of the far wall. On the right, there is a partition, with a water dispenser against and several things pinned on the wall partition. 

The back right of the room opens to another area, which Morena uses as an office. A couch sits on the other side of Morena's table, and behind Morena's office chair is a large TV with several game consoles and games. Heil-Ly underlings frequently speak with Morena here. This is also where Borksen regains consciousness, after being abducted by the Heil-Ly. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 407 

There appears to be a large room which Morena describes as "processing", which is not clearly physically connected to the Heil-Ly Secret Hideout. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 405 There is also a bunker-like corridor which has been frequently drawn, but how it connects to the Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout is unknown. 

During their attempt at infiltrating and identifying the location of the Heil-Ly Family Secret Hideout, Hinrigh is able to hide a transmitter, earlier transformed into a raw oyster using his Biohazard, below a bookshelf-cupboard in the laundry room. Nobunaga regroups with Phinks and Feitan, and they use the receiver Hinrigh gives them to (incorrectly) guess that the hideout is located somewhere on Tier 2 from their location on Tier 3. 

 
Chap 398 - Processing Room.png|Processing Room
Chap 394 - Disposal Room.png|Disposal Room
Chap 394 - Morena's Office.png|Morena's Office
Chap 399 - Communal Area.png|Communal Area
 

Tier 3

Tier 3 (3, San-sō) is in the middle portion of the Black Whale's hull. Like Tiers 4 and 5, it is zoned for general passengers. A thick bulkhead separates it from the upper tiers, and it can only be opened in emergencies from the second-tier side. There appear to be further partitions of its General Passenger Area into at least 5 sections as Areas C and E are mentioned by Cha-R Underboss Ken'i. It is unclear whether this naming system is used by Kakin or the general public, or if it's part of an internal naming system devised by the Cha-R in their search for Hisoka. Hisoka Actually Bonolenov disguised as Hisoka, under Chrollo's orders to be found and secure the Spiders freedom to find Hisoka themselves. is first found by the Xi-Yu in a cineplex on Tier 3. in a cineplex on Tier 3. 

The tier would have been controlled by the Heil-Ly Family, who gets their income by being intermediaries between the wealthy and the black market, until the Heil-Ly's new boss Morena ordered her members to wreak havoc and kill everyone on the ship. Like the other lower tiers of the Black Whale 1, Tier 3 does not have a great number of soldiers. This combined with the lack of mafia presence makes the tier one of if not the least secured tiers on the ship. On the other hand, the tier is the location of the medical and political wards, and has three clinics, the highest number in the lower tiers. 

Passenger Cabins

First-Class Cabins (, Ittō Kyakushitsu) and Standard Cabins (, Ippan Kyakushitsu) are both available in the highest tier zoned for general passengers. Near the top of Tier 3, a standard cabin consists of a bed, cupboards, shelves, and a bathroom. The single cabins are aligned in a corridor that connects to a General Passenger Area (エリア, Ippan Jōkyaku Eria) which acts as an assembly point. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 379 A corporal of the Royal Army describes the area as one where the noveau riche and all sorts of deplorables can blend in. 

Room 3101 (3101, 3101-gōshitsu) and Room 3131 (3131, 3131-gōshitsu) are first-class cabins owned and used by the Heil-Ly Family to capture and kill unsuspecting passengers in their secret hideout so that they can level up their abilities. The rooms also act as secret passageways for the members to travel to and from the hideout. Due to the declaration of special martial law, Room 3101 The weekly release of Chapter 410 references Room (3)125 in conversation, but a tweet from Togashi's X account on 21/12/2024 mentions that the room in reference during the conversation is meant to be Room 3101. This is intended to be corrected during the volume release of Chapter 410. is blocked off by the military. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 410 

 
Chap 393 - first-class cabins.png|First-Class Cabins
Chap 393 - floor plan.png|First-Class Cabin Floor Plan
single cabins.png|Standard Cabins
single cabin map.png|Standard Cabin Floor Plan
general passenger area.png|General Passenger Area
 

Political Ward

The Political Ward (, Seiji Tokku) is located in the middle portion of the tier. It serves as the main headquarters that contains the different Political and Legal Enforcement Agencies (・の, Seiji/Hōritsu no Shikō Kikan), the Central Police Station, and the Royal Army Office. 

One of the law enforcement agencies, the Central Courthouse (, Chūō Saibansho) maintains order on the Black Whale. It is headed by Botobai, who acts as the court's judge. The room is filled with soldiers from the Kakin Royal Army who keep order and lead the defendants to the courtroom, a clerk to keep the records, and what appears to be lay judges. 

The Central Police Station (, Chūō Keisatsu) is a four-story building headed by Mizaistom. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 Its dorm provides individual rooms as well as communal bathrooms for the police officers and other members of staff. One of its facilities, the Community Safety Section, serves as an interview room for non-detained visitors. 

The Royal Army Office (, Kokuō-gun Shibu) holds meetings between generals of the Kakin Royal Army and is headed by Mizaistom. During meetings, the committee members discuss issues regarding the public safety aboard the ship. Once they have agreed on certain measures, they have the authority to execute most of them unless further procedures are needed such as relaying the information to Prince Benjamin, the head of Kakin's national defense. 

 
central courthouse.png|Central Courthouse
central police station.png|Central Police Station
community safety section.png|Community Safety Section
Royal army office.png|Royal Army Office
 

Medical Ward

The Medical Ward (, Ika-gaku Tokku) is a special zone located in the middle of the tier that contains the Central Hospital (, Chūō Byōin) and the Research Institute (, Sōgōkenkyūjo). 

The central hospital accommodates at least one of the three clinics on the tier, the Central Medical Clinic (, Chūō Iryō-shitsu), which is supervised by Cheadle. 

 
Hunter × Hunter - v34 p173 - c359 - (VIZ) (Digital CC) (PZG).png|Central Medical Clinic
 

When Halkenburg collapsed on the eleventh day of the voyage, he was taken to a General Hospital (, Sōgō Byōin), where Cheadle, Leorio, and Gel were involved in treating him for some time. It is unclear where this General Hospital is, but it is very likely on Tier 3. Hunter × Hunter, Chapter 404 

Observation Deck

The Observation Deck (エリア, Tenbō Eria) is a sightseeing platform located at the front of the Black Whale. It is filled with numerous outdoor lounge chairs, shops, and bars. 

 
380 - Observation Deck.png|Observation Deck
 

Heil-Ly Family Office

The Heil-Ly Family Office (エイ＝イ, Ei i Ikka Jimusho) is located near the bottom of the tier and is the official location of the Heil-Ly family aboard the ship. The office was abandoned early into the voyage although it was occasionally used by members, and was eventually discovered to be abandoned after being raided by several soldiers. It has a passageway that connects to Morena's quarters on Tier 1. 

Movie Theatre

The Movie Theatre (シネマ, shinema or , gekijō) is a location on Tier 3, where Bonolenov (while transformed into Hisoka) watched a movie as he waited to be found by the Heil-Ly and convinced to be moved to the upper tiers. The Movie Theatre has at least 8 screens, and has shown the movie The Thing. Zakuro uses シネマ (shinema, translated by Viz as "cineplex") to refer to this location, whereas Bonolenov uses (gekijō, translated by Viz as "movie theatre") to refer to it. 

Tier 4

Tier 4 (4, Yon-sō) is in the middle portion of the Black Whale's hull. Like Tiers 3 and 5, it is zoned for general passengers. Although it appears to be the largest in terms of size, Tier 4 only has one medical clinic. 

The tier is controlled by the Xi-Yu Family, which profits from human trafficking and the distribution of goods. It also relies on them to maintain public order since the number of civilians for every guard and Royal Army soldier exceeds 300 in the lower tiers. 

Kakin Royal Army Conference Room

The Kakin Royal Army Conference Room (カキン, Kakin Kokuō-gun Kaigishitsu) is responsible for ensuring security on the Black Whale. It is the meeting point for people affiliated with the Royal Army and is currently headed by Mizaistom. 

 
conference room.png|Kakin Royal Army Conference Room
 

Xi-Yu Family Office

The Xi-Yu Family Office (シュウ＝ウ, Shu u Ikka Jimusho) is located in the front portion of the ship, within the "teeth" of the Black Whale. It acts as a hideout for the family's members. 

Between Tiers 4 and 5

Sewage Processing Facility

The sewage processing facility for the Black Whale 1's 200,000 passengers lies between Tiers 4 and 5 

Tier 5

Tier 5 (5, Go-sō) is located at the bottom of the Black Whale's hull, next to its propeller and completely below the ocean. Like Tiers 3 and 4, it is zoned for general passengers. It is the smallest of the lower tiers and has only one medical clinic with no dedicated doctors. 

The tier is controlled by the Cha-R Family, which is in charge of all commodities. It also relies on them to maintain public order since the number of civilians for every guard and Royal Army soldier exceeds 300 in the lower tiers. 

Passenger Cabins

Standard Cabins (, Ippan Kyakushitsu) are the only confirmed rooms to be available for the general passengers on the lowest tier. 

37564

37564 is an assembly point on Tier 5 where Chrollo was first seen on the ship. 

 
37564.png|37654
 

Central Dining Hall

The Central Dining Hall (, Chūō Shokudō) is the only cafeteria on Tier 5. It can only be accessed from one passageway that is controlled by three Buor Family thugs after they bribed the soldiers in charge. A passenger can pay them 5,000, borrow loyalty cards, or follow a payment schedule to gain access. After the thugs were subdued by several members of the Phantom Troupe, it is unknown if they still have authority over the passageway. 

 
Chap 377 - Central Dining Hall.png|Central Dining Hall
 

Warehouse

The Warehouse (, Sōko) is controlled by the Cha-R Family. It stores various goods, some of which were smuggled in from the black market. Four guards were positioned to watch over the warehouse before they were killed by Luini. The Cha-R Family also has one security camera installed at the warehouse's entrance, after all the cargo had been loaded. 

 
Warehouse.png|Warehouse
 

Cha-R Family Office

The Cha-R Family Office (シャ＝ア, Sha a Ikka Jimusho) is located in the middle section of the tier, right next to the ship's propeller. It acts as a hideout for the family's members. A steel door with a smart lock and two security cameras guard the entrance. The office consists of an office, a bedroom, and a monitor room that records surveillance over all the key areas of Tier 5. It also has a passageway that connects to Brocco Li's quarters on Tier 1. 

 
Chap 380 - Cha-R Family base.png|Cha-R Family Office
 

Trivia

37564 is a Japanese wordplay, which can be read "mi-na-go-ro-shi" (みなごろし), meaning "massacre" or "kill them all". 

Intertextuality and References

The division of classes on the Black Whale resembles that of the RMS Titanic, a British passenger ship notorious for its sinking in 1912. The classes were separated similarly, with the wealthier passengers being near the top while the poorer were closer to the bottom. RMS Titanic, Wikipedia contributors.
